title: Validate LLM-provided tool inputs against ToolSpec.input_schema before dispatch
current_state: agent/tools.py:execute_tool() at line ~223 calls spec.handler(agent, tool_input, **kwargs) without validating tool_input against spec.input_schema. If the LLM omits required keys (e.g. memory_read called without 'query'), the handler raises KeyError when it does tool_input['query']. The KeyError is caught upstream as a generic 'error' dict that doesn't tell the LLM what is missing, so it cannot self-correct on the next turn.
structural_problem: Every tool handler has to do its own input checking. Most don't. The LLM gets opaque error messages instead of schema-aware feedback, so it tends to retry the same broken call rather than fixing the inputs.
target_state: execute_tool() validates tool_input against spec.input_schema (JSON Schema) BEFORE calling the handler. On mismatch, returns a structured error: {'error': 'invalid_input', 'schema_mismatch': [...field-level errors...], 'expected_schema': spec.input_schema}. The schema_mismatch + expected_schema fields let the LLM self-correct.
design_notes: Use python jsonschema package (small, stable, no transitive deps that conflict with Pi's stack). If already a transitive dep via supabase, no requirements.txt change needed.
Validation should be FAST â€” schemas are small (a handful of keys); cache the validator object per ToolSpec on first use
Schema mismatch error format: {'error': 'invalid_input', 'tool': tool_name, 'schema_mismatch': [{'path': 'query', 'message': 'required field missing'}, ...], 'expected_schema': spec.input_schema}
Validation must NOT mutate tool_input (no coercion). Reject loud, do not silently fix.
Allow tools to opt OUT of validation via ToolSpec.validate_input: bool = True attribute, in case some tool has dynamic schemas â€” likely not needed initially but cheap to add

When validation fails, call track_silent('tools.invalid_input', ValueError(error_msg), context={'tool': tool_name, 'errors': errors}) so we can see if a tool's schema needs revision (e.g. LLM consistently mis-shapes the call)
Validation error MUST NOT raise out of execute_tool â€” return as dict like any other tool error
success_criteria: memory_read called with {} (no query) returns {'error': 'invalid_input', 'tool': 'memory_read', 'schema_mismatch': [...query...]} â€” does NOT raise
memory_read called with valid {'query': 'foo'} executes normally (no regression)
memory_write called with tier='l5' (invalid enum) returns schema_mismatch listing the enum violation
Schema validator is cached per ToolSpec (assert via mock that compile is called once per spec across many invocations)
track_silent records 'tools.invalid_input' on each validation failure
verify.py PASS â€” all 734 existing tests still PASS (no test currently passes invalid input intentionally; if any do they need updating)
risk_notes: Risk: existing tests may inadvertently pass partial inputs that previously 'worked' because the handler tolerated missing keys via .get(). Mitigation: run full test suite; if any failures, decide case-by-case whether to fix the test or relax the schema.
migration_plan: 1. Verify jsonschema is importable: 'python -c "import jsonschema"'. If missing, add to requirements.txt.
2. Add agent/tool_spec.py helper: _get_validator(spec) -> jsonschema.Draft7Validator, cached via @functools.lru_cache or instance attribute
3. Edit agent/tools.py:execute_tool â€” after the 'spec = _TOOLS_BY_NAME.get(tool_name)' check, add validation block. If errors, return structured dict and call track_silent.
4. Add testing/test_tool_input_validation.py covering: missing required key, wrong type, invalid enum, valid input passes through, caching
5. Run scripts/verify.py â€” investigate any test regressions; fix the test or schema as appropriate
6. Update PI.md Â§6 (or docs/adr/) noting that LLM tool inputs are now schema-validated; bad inputs return structured errors the LLM can self-correct from
test_plan: test_wrong_type_returns_structured_error â€” memory_read({'query': 123}) â€” query expected string
test_invalid_enum_value â€” memory_write({'content': 'x', 'tier': 'l99'}) â€” l99 not in enum
test_valid_input_passes_through â€” memory_read({'query': 'foo'}) returns expected result (mock memory)
test_validator_cached â€” call execute_tool 100x; assert jsonschema.Draft7Validator called once per unique spec
test_validation_failure_logged â€” call with bad input; assert track_silent recorded 'tools.invalid_input'
